steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says Jug found tilling increased wheat yield, then signals 'But some studies of other crops have found the opposite effect.' The correct choice must show no-till outperforming till for a crop other than wheat.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Min Huang and colleagues reported an even larger positive effect of no tilling on the yield of rice.: A different crop (rice) where no-till is better, directly opposing Jug's pro-tilling finding. ✓
  • Choice B — Bharat Sharma Acharya and colleagues found a similar association in a study using soybeans.: Same direction as Jug (tilling is better), just a different crop. Not the 'opposite effect.' ✗
  • Choice C — crop yields with tilling have ranged from 2,420 kilograms per hectare for maize to 4,860 kilograms per hectare for winter wheat.: No effect direction is established, so no 'opposite effect' can be inferred. ✗
  • Choice D — a study using winter wheat yielded 4,860 kilograms per hectare with tilling and only 3,910 kilograms per hectare without tilling.: Same crop and same direction as Jug. No contrast at all. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
